title: Chapter Ninety-Nine: Beautiful Hands
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

"Identity, and business."

Yeon So-ha stepped forward to face the girl. As the youngest of the Divine Sword Squad, dirty work fell to him. His briefly thrown words seemed almost rude, but no one minded.

*Having to appear strong to any martial arts sect...*

Yeon-shin took in the Squad anew. Yeon So-ha at the front with ruffian-like ease, the Group Leader looking as if out for a stroll, Yue Shou-lin with one leg planted and hands behind her back. No solemn momentum -- but more threatening than anyone. Twenty warriors containing power forged through countless battlefields.

"I want to let you know that we have no intention of being hostile," the girl said, bowing. Her composure before Desolate Fortress' premier fighting force was rare courage.

"And the young master?"

"I asked first? Showing your evil sect's true colours?"

"Now I see. They said there was an exceptionally sharp broom in the Divine Sword Squad. You must be Divine Sword Sharp Reed Yeon So-ha."

"You've guessed the title I hate. Are you testing my patience?"

"I apologize. Clear those away."

The last words were not directed at Yeon So-ha. Five or six blood demons who had completely lost their minds were charging in -- broken away from the Blood Flame Sect when it changed course. Eyes rolling with red light. Tongues hanging. Targeting Yeon-shin.

*I remember. Faces from the Blood Flame Sect main hall. They must have been away the day of the attack.*

Their dull mouths shaped words. *Great Teacher, Great Teacher, please protect us...*

The girl -- Hwa Yeon-bi -- murmured a single word. "Clear."

That light command caused a sharp ripple. Masters immediately rushed out, weapons remarkable, martial arts from half a dozen traditions blended together. The blood demons died in seconds.

One guard returned with a long cut on his arm. The girl smiled silently at his wound.

"Your achievement is insufficient."

"I will train harder."

No anger. No other colour to the exchange. It would have been more comfortable if there were.

"Thanks for handling that," Yeon So-ha said. "So -- name and position?"

"Hwa Yeon-bi."

One answer to two questions. Her name was her position.

"I came to see the Divine Sword Group Leader's face," she continued. "The duel with the Namgung Family Head was truly impressive. I pay my respects to the Group Leader. Anyone would desperately want to see you."

"No other intentions? Like wanting to test things out?"

"Our association purely reveres martial arts. How could we act rashly? Before the Divine Sword Group Leader, who stands right below the peak of martial arts under heaven."

"Below the peak?"

"The world's finest is the Desolate Fortress Lord..."

"Martial Dragon Association." The Group Leader's voice cut in. Hwa Yeon-bi's face showed tension for the first time. "The main fortress' Lord is not someone for the likes of you to discuss as a martial artist. How dare you place Desolate Fortress' master on the ambitions of murim rabble? If discussing the world's finest in martial ways, I alone am enough."

Outrageous. He claimed to be the finest himself. But it felt natural.

"...Hey, youngster." Yue Shou-lin had drifted to Yeon-shin's side. "Feels like she has other business." Ferocity in her faint smile. "I already know. She was suggestively looking over our Lightning Genius. Quite blatant about it."

"Ah." Hwa Yeon-bi flushed slightly. Her expression suggested she had been waiting for those exact words. "That person. You called him Lightning Genius? I saw an amazing duel. So I wanted to invite him. To our association."

Yeon-shin's expression shifted. Great disappointment seemed to pound from his heart to his middle dantian.

"Of course I don't dare suggest leaving Desolate Fortress for an evil sect. Just--"

"If your business is solely with me."

He turned his head. His words were directed at Yue Shou-lin and the Group Leader. He no longer paid attention to Hwa Yeon-bi.

"We should continue our journey. Having completed the mission, we should return quickly."

"...Oh? Right?"

The Group Leader answered bewilderedly. Yeon-shin nodded.

"We have nothing particular to see either. Let's continue. The former leader agrees?"

"...Yes. Take the lead for this journey."

Ma Yeon-jeok answered briefly, glaring at the Martial Dragon Association group. Displeased that Hwa Yeon-bi had mentioned his grandson.

**Step.**

They moved forward. The Divine Sword Squad paid no attention to the potential enemies. The entire group passed the Martial Dragon Association without a backward glance. Hwa Yeon-bi's stare was piercing, but Yeon-shin closed his eyes. Organizing the insights gained from the Group Leader's body. He could walk even while doing so.

When even the landslide's aftermath had died far behind them, a familiar body scent wafted over. Yue Shou-lin.

"Hey, hey. What's this? Why did you do that? Such a pretty child said she saw your martial might and wanted to invite you."

"...Hands."

"Hm?"

He opened his eyes slowly.

"They were beautiful hands. Yet she spoke of her subordinate's achievement being insufficient."

"Ah." Yue Shou-lin's lips curved. *Well done.*

"Peerless swordsmen carry famine in their palms -- calluses. Not all martial arts leave traces on the practitioner's hands, but the ones I've learned do. I wasn't curious about her martial arts. If Hwa Yeon-bi was born with natural abilities, then I have nothing to learn from her. And if she reversed aging, it means she's been lazy in training since then -- even less reason. The world's martial arts constantly develop. Rather than responding to such a person, it seemed better to seek teachings from Senior Yue."

"You really are interesting."

Just as her mouth corners rose further -- the Divine Sword Group Leader stopped walking.

"Is anyone around? Everyone, speak up. Quickly."

"No. Doesn't seem like it."

Right after Yeon So-ha's reply--

The Divine Sword Group Leader violently vomited blood. A considerable amount. Black blood poured from his mouth like water drawn from a mountain spring with a ladle. Evidence of internal injury -- the natural price of defeating the Namgung Family Head in a short decisive battle.

**WHOOSH.**

The Divine Sword Squad masters formed a protective circle with instinctive precision.

"Namgung Family Head. Strongest opponent in nearly three years," Yue Shou-lin said, shaking her head. Worry on her small face.

The Group Leader dropped to both knees. Blood he had been holding back, changing from black to red. Even Yeon-shin could only watch cautiously.

"If you've been holding back until now, it was right not to confront the Martial Dragon Association," the boy said slowly. "Even if they grow suspicious, that decision was correct. The Sect Leader's body is the main fortress' treasure. Thinking of your body--"

"Lightning Genius? What are you saying? No matter how much I push myself." The Group Leader smiled with blood-stained lips. For a moment, his eyes flashed with light.

"I'm not the only one."

---
